verdict_summary: Plan's structural fix-shape is correct and verifiable at file:line. RED → GREEN transition for cpp_deserialization_positive will happen as planned. However, the plan contains a localized SPEC DEFECT in its description of WHERE the descend-through set is extended (it lives in the OUTER helper, not the inner descent helper), AND a numerical FALSE CLAIM about fixture count (18 vs verified 13). Neither blocks the underlying engineering, but both will trip the implementing kraken if the prompt is taken literally. CONDITIONAL on amendments A1 + A2 below being applied to the plan/contract or the implementation prompt before dispatch.

specific_validations_performed.claim: tree-sitter-cpp parses fixture L7 as `declaration → init_declarator → argument_list → binary_expression → call_expression(std::stringstream) → identifier(d)`
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: Read fixture crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/cpp/deserialization_positive.cpp ✓ confirmed text matches investigation.json. Predecessor's premortem (88f5620) directly invoked tree_sitter::Parser to capture this shape. Investigation.json L37-L48 records key_observations. Trusted as VERIFIED — no contrary evidence in current source.

specific_validations_performed.claim: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent (added by var-extract-nested-constructor-v1, commit b577796) is reusable as-is for cpp arm
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: Read taint.rs L4170-4234. Helper signature `fn extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent(node: &tree_sitter::Node, source: &[u8], language: Language, depth: u32) -> Option<String>` confirmed. Body is unconditional BFS over all named descendants with depth bound (MAX_DEPTH=5 at L4186) and per-level string-kind filter (L4208). The descent helper does NOT consult any per-language descend-through set internally — it walks all named children. Under the planned cpp arm, invoking it on the `argument_list` node will BFS-traverse → binary_expression → call_expression(std::stringstream) → argument_list → identifier("d") and return Some("d").
specific_validations_performed.verdict: ✓ VERIFIED — reusable as-is. NOTE: the descent helper itself has no per-language extension point; the descend-through `match` resides in the OUTER helper at L4117-4129. See discrepancy DI-1 below.

specific_validations_performed.claim: CPP_AST_SINKS L2658 already has `boost::archive::text_iarchive` Deserialize entry
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: Read taint.rs L2629-2670. CPP_AST_SINKS Deserialize entry at L2655-2662: `member_patterns: &[("", "boost::archive::text_iarchive"), ("", "cereal::BinaryInputArchive")], sink_type: TaintSinkType::Deserialize`. Both entries are present at the cited line range.

specific_validations_performed.claim: string-kind filter applies at every recursion level in the descent helper
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: Read taint.rs L4203-4210. While loop pops (cur, d). Line 4208: `if string_kinds.contains(&cur.kind()) { continue; }` — applied BEFORE the identifier-leaf attempt and BEFORE pushing children. closes-#24 regression-guard preserved by construction.

specific_validations_performed.claim: Cpp arm is `language == Cpp && descendant.kind() == "declaration"` keyed; no other languages have meaningful `declaration` shapes that would accidentally match
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: ast_utils.rs L60-L82: only C and Cpp have `declaration` in assignment_node_kinds. No other language uses `declaration` as a node kind in any helper consulted by extract_first_identifier_arg_ast. The Cpp arm's `language == Language::Cpp` guard is sufficient — no cross-language pollution risk.

specific_validations_performed.claim: M3 GREEN-regression sweep includes 18 string_literal_fp fixtures + 167/168 currently-GREEN tests
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: Counted fixtures at file system: `find crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/ -name 'deserialization_string_literal_fp.*'` returns 13 files (cpp, csharp, elixir, java, javascript, kotlin, ocaml, php, python, ruby, rust, scala, typescript). The plan's claim of 18 is UNSOURCED. Total `*_string_literal_fp.*` = 84 (all vuln types × languages). The 18 is neither subset is consistent. Likely typo; intended count is 13 (deserialization-specific) per dispatch-contract m3_binary_smoke_string_literal_fp wording.
specific_validations_performed.verdict: ✗ FALSE CLAIM — plan §M3 and §4 R3 say 18; actual is 13. Discrepancy DI-2.

specific_validations_performed.claim: Predecessor commits + tags exist; var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 deferred Cpp scope per amendment A1
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: git log shows commit b577796 `feat(taint): nested-constructor descent in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (var-extract-nested-constructor-v1)`, commit 88f5620 `docs(var-extract-nested-constructor-v1): discriminative premortem report`, commit 411715b `docs(var-extract-nested-constructor-v1): incorporate premortem amendments`. taint.rs L4107-L4116 documents the deferral inline.

specific_validations_performed.claim: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast L3945+ has language-specific arms placed before generic args lookup; new Cpp arm placement is structurally consistent
specific_validations_performed.verification_method: Read L3945-L4168. Confirmed PHP echo_statement (L3959-3994), Ruby subshell (L4013-4036), OCaml application_expression (L4043-4070) precede generic args lookup at L4076. New Cpp arm fits this pattern.

tigers.description: Plan's mechanism description references the WRONG helper for the descend-through extension. Plan §1 item 2 and §2 M2 say `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent`'s per-language descend-through set lives at L4180+. VERIFIED: the per-language `descend_kinds` match arm lives at L4117-4129 INSIDE THE OUTER helper `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast`. The inner descent helper at L4180+ contains no per-language match. An implementing kraken following the plan literally may add a Cpp arm to the wrong helper or to a non-existent extension point in the descent helper, causing M2 to either no-op or fail clippy.
tigers.blast_radius: M2 implementation derail — kraken edits the wrong location, M2 stop-threshold `cpp_deserialization_positive transitions RED → GREEN` does not trip.
tigers.mitigation: AMENDMENT A1: clarify in plan §1 item 2 / §2 M2 / dispatch-contract.json::milestones[1].green_files[0].additions that the per-language descend-through set extension is at `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast` taint.rs L4117-4129 (NOT inside the descent helper). The descent helper itself stays unchanged — the Cpp arm in the OUTER helper short-circuits to `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent(value=argument_list, ...)` directly (NOT routed through the L4117-4129 descend chain at all, because the entry arm fires before the args-list lookup at L4076). RECONSIDER: does the plan even need the L4117-4129 Cpp extension? Under Option A's actual flow, the new entry arm bypasses L4076 entirely (early `return None` if init_declarator/value missing, else delegate to descent). The L4117-4129 extension only fires for cpp `call_expression` sinks where the `argument_list` post-fields-lookup contains a constructor-shaped child — a DIFFERENT scenario from the deserialization fixture. So the L4117-4129 extension is COSMETIC for cpp_deserialization_positive but PROVIDES PROTECTION AGAINST FUTURE Cpp call_expression nested-constructor cases. Recommend keep the extension AND clarify the documentation.
tigers.verdict_impact: Conditional-pass. A1 is a documentation/scope-clarification amendment, not an engineering change.

tigers.description: Plan §M3 claims 18 string_literal_fp fixtures; verified count is 13 (deserialization-specific) or 84 (all-vuln-types). The dispatch-contract m3_binary_smoke_string_literal_fp mandate uses glob `*/deserialization_string_literal_fp.*` which matches 13. M3 stop-threshold `All 18 *_string_literal_fp fixtures yield 0 findings` is unmeetable as written; orchestrator/kraken will hit a stop-threshold mismatch.
tigers.blast_radius: M3 verification false-failure or scope confusion at tag time.
tigers.mitigation: AMENDMENT A2: correct plan §M3 binary smoke step + §4 R3 mitigation note + §6 self-validation to read `13` (deserialization-specific) instead of `18`. Dispatch-contract.json::milestones[2].verification_steps[6] glob is correct (`*/deserialization_string_literal_fp.*`); only the prose count is wrong.
tigers.verdict_impact: Conditional-pass. A2 is a number correction.

tigers.description: Cpp arm fires on UNRELATED `declaration` nodes that match other CPP_AST_SINKS (FileOpen std::ifstream / std::ofstream typed-local-decl shape). Investigation.json::scope_for_other_cpp_sinks correctly notes std::ifstream/std::ofstream use the SAME `declaration` shape, so the new arm will run on those too. If a future cpp/path_traversal_positive fixture uses `std::ifstream f(d);`, the arm now fires AND var-extraction succeeds, which is desirable for true positives, but if any currently-GREEN cpp test relies on var-extraction returning None for a typed-local-decl declaration shape, M3 sweep can flip that test RED→GREEN→FAIL (assertion mismatch).
tigers.blast_radius: M3 cross-fixture regression on currently-GREEN cpp fixtures asserting `expected ZERO findings` (e.g., cpp string_literal_fp fixtures that incidentally parse as declaration with init_declarator).
tigers.mitigation: Investigation.json L107-110 cites that descent helper's per-level string-kind filter PLUS the outer is_in_string filter at detect_sinks_ast L4431 already excludes string-wrapping ancestors. R1 + R3 in plan §4 are explicitly mitigated by M3 sweep gating. Kraken MUST NOT skip M3 stop-threshold `All 167 currently-GREEN tests at HEAD remain GREEN`. ANY GREEN→RED transition triggers M2 revert + root-cause investigation per dispatch-contract m3_red_count_delta_assertion mandate. Treat as monitored TIGER, not blocker.
tigers.verdict_impact: Acceptable risk under plan's stated mitigation. M3 sweep is gating.

elephants.description: Plan's Option A var-extraction trace (§3 step 5/6/7) describes the descent as 'traversing through descend-through set' — but the descent helper does NOT gate on a per-language descend-through set. It does an unconditional BFS over all named descendants (depth-bounded, string-filtered). The trace works regardless because the BFS finds `identifier(d)` first via leftmost source order. So the documented mechanism is wrong but the predicted outcome is correct.
elephants.mitigation: AMENDMENT A1 (subsumes): rewrite §3 trace to describe unconditional BFS over named descendants of the argument_list. Engineering outcome unchanged.

elephants.description: Tree-sitter-cpp grammar version drift. Plan R4 covers this. Pinned 0.23.4 per Cargo.lock. If init_declarator or argument_list renames in a future bump, arm fails closed (returns None) and fixture re-RED would surface in CI. Not actionable now.

Single-source-file edit (taint.rs) interacts with predecessor var-extract-nested-constructor-v1's existing per-language descend-through set. The L4117-4129 Cpp extension (`binary_expression`, `call_expression`, `parenthesized_expression`, `argument_list`) is COSMETIC for cpp_deserialization_positive (because the new entry arm short-circuits before reaching the args-list scan), but PROVIDES PROTECTION for future Cpp sink-shape variants where the helper is invoked on a `call_expression` whose first arg is a nested constructor-shape. If that extension is omitted (or misplaced), the deserialization fixture STILL turns GREEN — but a class of forward Cpp fixtures may stay broken. Recommend keeping the extension as documented, noting in M2 doc-comment that it's forward-coverage.

amendments_required.change: Clarify that the per-language descend-through set extension is to the match arm AT L4117-4129 INSIDE extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (the OUTER helper), NOT inside extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent (the inner descent helper, which has no per-language extension point). The new Cpp entry arm in the outer helper short-circuits to invoke the descent helper directly on argument_list and returns; this bypasses the L4117-4129 extension for the deserialization fixture's flow. The L4117-4129 Cpp extension is forward-coverage for future Cpp call_expression nested-constructor sinks. Rewrite §3 trace step 5-7 to describe unconditional BFS over named descendants (no per-language gating in the descent helper).
amendments_required.rationale: Prevents M2 implementer from editing the wrong helper or assuming the L4117-4129 extension is consulted on the deserialization fixture's flow.

target_files: continuum/autonomous/cpp-deser-declaration-v1-plan/plan.md (§2 M3 binary smoke step; §4 R3 mitigation; §6 self-validation)
amendments_required.change: Correct fixture count from 18 to 13 (matches `find <fixture-root> -name 'deserialization_string_literal_fp.*' | wc -l` = 13). Dispatch-contract.json glob is already correct at `*/deserialization_string_literal_fp.*`.
amendments_required.rationale: Prevents stop-threshold mismatch at M3. Cosmetic but visible to validator.

ready_conditional_on: A1 applied to plan/contract OR injected into M2 prompt as override
dispatch_readiness.rationale: All structural claims verifiable at file:line. Fix-shape is structurally correct: a new Cpp entry arm in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (placed before the L4076 args-list lookup) that walks declaration → init_declarator → child_by_field_name(value=argument_list) → invokes extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent. The descent helper's existing string-kind filter at L4208 preserves closes-#24 by construction. CPP_AST_SINKS bank is correct as cited (L2658). M3 GREEN-regression sweep is the gating safety net and the dispatch-contract mandates it correctly. Two cosmetic/documentation defects (A1, A2) need amendment but neither blocks the underlying engineering. RED → GREEN transition for cpp_deserialization_positive is reachable as planned.
dispatch_readiness.expected_red_count_delta: vuln_migration_v1_red: 1 → 0 (167/168 GREEN → 168/168 GREEN). Closes the LAST remaining vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forward.

discrepancy_register.claim: Per-language descend-through set extension lives at L4180+ inside extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent
discrepancy_register.actual: Per-language descend-through `descend_kinds` match arm lives at L4117-4129 inside OUTER helper extract_first_identifier_arg_ast. Inner descent helper has no per-language extension point; it does unconditional BFS over named descendants with string-kind filter at every level.
discrepancy_register.behavioral_impact: None for cpp_deserialization_positive (entry arm short-circuits before L4076). The L4117-4129 Cpp extension is forward-coverage only.

discrepancy_register.claim: 18 string_literal_fp fixtures gated in M3 sweep
discrepancy_register.behavioral_impact: M3 stop-threshold prose mismatch; dispatch-contract glob is correct.

discrepancy_register.claim: Plan §3 trace step 5/6/7 describes descent helper traversing via descend-through set
discrepancy_register.actual: Descent helper does unconditional BFS over named descendants. The descend-through set is consulted only in the outer helper. Trace's outcome (returns Some("d")) is correct; mechanism description is wrong.

predecessor_pattern_check.pattern_consistency: This premortem is consistent with the prior 7 in surfacing pre-dispatch defects. Two MEDIUM/LOW amendments (A1 documentation, A2 numerical) — within the typical amendment count for a CONDITIONAL-PASS verdict (cf. var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 which carried amendment A1 deferring Cpp scope, becoming THIS milestone).
